Interface AudioManager

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable, NonThrowingAutoCloseable
All Known Implementing Classes:
OpenALAudioManager
public interface AudioManager extends NonThrowingAutoCloseable
An audio manager is responsible for sound loading and managing sound instances.

Note: Do not manually close sound instance objects returned by an audio manager. Instead, use the destroySoundInstance(String) method for proper cleanup.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    close()

    Array bulk version of loadSound(Path).

    tick()
    Advances the audio manager by one tick.

  • Method Details

    • loadSound

      void loadSound(Path path) throws IOException
      Loads a sound from file at the given path. If a sound is already loaded at the given path, it is closed, removed and replaced with a new one. The sprite/stage assets are loaded automatically, so there's usually no need to call this method manually.

      See SoundDecoder for supported formats list.

      Parameters:
      path - the path to the sound file
      Throws:
      IOException - if something goes wrong during loading, or if the file format isn't recognized
      See Also:
      • loadSounds(Path...)
      • loadSounds(Iterable)
      • SoundDecoder

    • loadSounds

      default void loadSounds(Path... paths) throws IOException
      Array bulk version of loadSound(Path). The sprite/stage assets are loaded automatically, so there's usually no need to call this method manually.
      Parameters:
      paths - the paths to sound files
      Throws:
      IOException - if something goes wrong during loading, or if the file format isn't recognized
      See Also:
      • loadSound(Path)
      "Implementation Note:"
      By default, if loading of a sound file fails, this method will continue loading other files and then throw the exception. If multiple sound files fail to load, their exceptions will get suppressed into one.

    • loadSounds

      default void loadSounds(Iterable<? extends Path> paths) throws IOException
      Iterable bulk version of loadSound(Path). The sprite/stage assets are loaded automatically, so there's usually no need to call this method manually.
      Parameters:
      paths - the paths to sound files
      Throws:
      IOException - if something goes wrong during loading, or if the file format isn't recognized
      See Also:
      • loadSound(Path)
      "Implementation Note:"
      By default, if loading of a sound file fails, this method will continue loading other files and then throw the exception. If multiple sound files fail to load, their exceptions will get suppressed into one.

    • hasLoadedSound

      boolean hasLoadedSound(Path path)
      Determines whether a sound is already loaded from file at the given path. This can be useful to prevent unnecessary sound reloading. This is always true for sprite/stage assets since they're loaded automatically.
      Parameters:
      path - the file path
      Returns:
      true if a sound from file at the given path is already loaded by this manager, false otherwise.

    • createStaticSoundInstance

      it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.Pair<String, ? extends SoundInstance<?>> createStaticSoundInstance(Path filePath)
      Creates a new sound instance from the loaded sound at the given path.

      This is ideal for short sounds like sound effects or UI feedback that should load quickly and play immediately.

      A sound must be loaded at the given path using loadSound(Path) before calling this method. (For sprite/stage assets, this is done automatically.)

      Note for Scratchers: "Static" sound instances are similar to how all sounds work in Scratch. The sound is fully loaded into memory, so it starts playing instantly and can be played multiple times efficiently.

      To play a sound just like it's done in Scratch (from a sprite's assets, one-shot), it's better to use the startSound(...) methods of Sprite. They handle the sound instance's lifecycle automatically.

      Parameters:
      filePath - the path to the (already loaded!) sound file
      Returns:
      a Pair containing:
      • Key: The unique ID of the new sound instance
      • Value: The sound instance itself, ready to play
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if no sound is loaded at the given path
      IllegalStateException - if this audio manager is closed

    • createStreamingSoundInstance

      it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.Pair<String, ? extends SoundInstance<?>> createStreamingSoundInstance(Path filePath, boolean loop)
      Creates a new "streaming" sound instance from the loaded sound at the given path.

      Streaming sound instances are dynamic; they don't store their data all at once but instead request chunks on demand. This works well with long ambient music tracks, since they don't get loaded entirely immediately, which would cause lags.

      A sound must be loaded at the given path using loadSound(Path) before calling this method. (For sprite/stage assets, this is done automatically.)

      Note for Scratchers: This is different from how sounds work in Scratch. In Scratch, all sounds are loaded fully before playing. Here, streaming allows very long sounds (like background music) to play without delay and with low memory usage. Use this for tracks longer than ~30 seconds.

      Parameters:
      filePath - the path to the (already loaded!) sound file
      loop - whether the sound instance must loop
      Returns:
      a Pair containing:
      • Key: The unique ID of the new sound instance
      • Value: The sound instance itself, ready to play
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if no sound is loaded at the given path
      IllegalStateException - if this audio manager is closed

    • getSoundInstance

      SoundInstance<?> getSoundInstance(String id)
      Returns a sound source by the given ID, if any, otherwise returns null.
      Parameters:
      id - the ID
      Returns:
      the respective sound source or null
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the audio manager is closed

    • destroySoundInstance

      void destroySoundInstance(String id)
      If a sound source with the given ID exists, stops, closes and removes it, otherwise does nothing.
      Parameters:
      id - the ID
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the audio manager is closed

    • tick

      void tick()
      Advances the audio manager by one tick. This method is called regularly from the audio loop, no need to call in manually in your code.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the audio manager is closed

    • close

      void close()
      Destroys this audio manager. The manager can not be used after call to this method. Calling this method more than once does not have any effect.
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface NonThrowingAutoCloseable
